text: a-0036's load-bearing real-body correction — that ~31% of inline-able haves invert compression direction once residual references are observable — was measured at the per-HAVE granularity (4/13), but q-0008's length covariate and a-0010's 'rough serialization is shorter' premise operate on the DECL serialization (the SFT training example is the whole proof). e-0039 lifts e-0038's per-have rate to the decl level over the same 7 real-body corpus_v3 decls. Result: 2/6 inline-able decls (33%) are pi_root-LONGER at the decl level — the same order as the per-have 4/13, so the inversion DOES carry to the training-example unit and the 'rough is shorter' assumption genuinely fails there for ~1/3 of granularity-bearing decls. BUT the failure is HEAVY-TAILED, not uniform: decl-level net (chars) is [-157,-72,-71,-6,+8,+189], median -38.5 (rough still shorter for the median decl); the single biggest inversion (+189) is 100% driven by ONE multiply-referenced 305-char body, and a decl with a 3x-referenced inverting have (Lattice.mk', worst +72) still ends NET-SHORTER (-6) via sibling compression. So per-have inversion does not always carry up, and decl-level inversion magnitude is concentrated in a few large multiply-referenced bodies. Consequence: q-0008's length covariate must model an opposite-sign, heavy-tailed decl-level tail (order tens-of-percent of granularity-bearing decls, not ~1%), dominated by a few large multi-ref haves — reinforcing a-0036's point (3) that the pi_root inliner must share (let/re-derive) rather than blind-duplicate. CAVEAT: same N=7 foundational subset as e-0038 (corpus_v2 full_proofs byte-identical, 0 incremental decls — the n is capped by the on-disk modules); 6 inline-able decls anchors the unit/direction, not a corpus-scale rate; heuristic parse, textual refs, crude duplication model; corpus-scale still needs e-0037's Modal run. (refines a-0036; also addresses q-0007's deep-retrain length-confound scoping.)
